gmiDescription: GMI +2 this year (no roll — boom years are flat).

effects.description: ENERGY assets: add +1 to every value update die result this year. Energy demand drives the broader expansion.

flavourText: Demand outpaces supply. Everyone's drilling.

effects.description: TECHNOLOGY assets: add +1 to every value update die result this year. Tech multiples expand with the market.

flavourText: Every portfolio is a tech portfolio this year.

effects.description: REAL_ESTATE assets: add +1 to every value update die result this year. Property values surge across all segments.

flavourText: They're not building any more land.

effects.description: FINANCE assets: add +1 to every value update die result this year. Credit conditions are loose. Everything is fundable.

flavourText: Rates are low. Ambition is high. What could go wrong.

effects.description: MANUFACTURING assets: add +1 to every value update die result this year. Orders are strong. Factories running at capacity.

flavourText: The machines don't stop. Neither do the margins.

effects.description: MEDIA_ENTERTAINMENT assets: add +1 to every value update die result AND treat this year's buzz die as HIGH buzz regardless of roll. The culture is paying attention.

flavourText: Everyone is watching. Everyone is spending. Everyone is famous.

gmiDescription: GMI +4 base. Roll d6: 1–2 = –1, 3–4 = +0, 5–6 = +1. Final GMI is +3, +4, or +5.

effects.description: Immediately refresh the entire market — discard all current cards and replace them. The bull market brings new opportunities.
All loan repayment rolls this year: trigger threshold one lower (e.g. triggers on 1–2 now triggers only on 1). Lender confidence is high.

flavourText: Everyone is a genius in a bull market.

gmiDescription: GMI +4 base. Roll d6: 1–2 = –1, 3–4 = +0, 5–6 = +1. Final GMI is +3, +4, or +5.

effects.description: All players gain +1 stress. The market is euphoric — ambition outpaces caution. Everyone is overextended.

flavourText: The valuations make no sense. Everyone buys more anyway.

effects.description: No secondary effect. A perfectly ordinary year.

flavourText: Nothing happened. The markets were fine.

effects.description: No secondary effect. GDP growth is positive but unexciting.

flavourText: The economy expanded. Nobody got a bonus.

effects.description: No secondary effect. A quiet year with no major events.

flavourText: Nothing to report. The spreadsheets updated themselves.

effects.description: Before the auction, all players may look at the top 3 cards of the market deck in order. Analyst reports are in — no secrets this year.

flavourText: The analyst predicted it. Nobody listened. Then everyone did.

effects.description: All loan repayment rolls this year: trigger threshold one lower. Lenders are lenient. Credit conditions are accommodating.

flavourText: Nobody is calling in favours this year.

effects.description: TECHNOLOGY assets: add +1 to every value update die result this year. A modest innovation cycle drives modest multiple expansion.

flavourText: A new framework was released. Investors approved.

effects.description: MANUFACTURING UPSTREAM and MIDSTREAM assets: add +1 to every value update die result. Supply chain investment drives input demand.

flavourText: Someone ordered a lot of concrete.

effects.description: REAL_ESTATE assets: loan capacity +2 this year only. Low rates drive cap rate compression — property is more bankable.

flavourText: Rates fell. Prices rose. The math somehow worked.

effects.description: No secondary effect. Nothing remarkable occurred. Analysts upgraded their outlooks for next year.

flavourText: At least it wasn't a bad year.

effects.description: REAL_ESTATE DOWNSTREAM assets: income counts as 1 higher for tax offset purposes only this year. Renters are paying. Vacancy rates are low.

flavourText: People feel good about the future. Some of them even mean it.

effects.description: No secondary effect. Everything is waiting for a catalyst.

flavourText: The market is closed. Spiritually.

effects.description: No secondary effect. A temporary reversal after a decline. Don't read into it.

flavourText: It went up a little. It will go back down.

effects.description: The market does NOT refresh this year — all current market cards remain in place for the auction. What you see is what you get.

flavourText: New issuances paused. Existing inventory only.

effects.description: Any player with more than 5 total loan tokens makes one additional repayment roll this year. Lenders are reviewing their exposure.

flavourText: The banks are reading their own fine print.

effects.description: Volatility suppressed: value update rolls of 1 are treated as 2, and rolls of 6 are treated as 5. The market is range-bound.

flavourText: Two years of trading and no one went anywhere.

effects.description: All loan repayment rolls this year: trigger threshold one higher. Regulatory ambiguity increases lender caution.

flavourText: No one knows what the rules are. Everyone is lending anyway.

effects.description: MEDIA_ENTERTAINMENT assets: subtract 1 from every value update die result this year (minimum 1). Media multiples contract faster than the broader market.

flavourText: Money moved. It moved away from you.

effects.description: All loan repayment rolls this year: trigger threshold one higher. Rising rates increase debt service pressure across all portfolios.

flavourText: The central bank met. Nobody was happy about it.

effects.description: TECHNOLOGY assets: subtract 1 from every value update die result (minimum 1). Tech leads the correction — high multiple assets fall fastest.

flavourText: Profitability is suddenly relevant again.

effects.description: REAL_ESTATE assets: loan capacity –2 this year only. Tightening lending standards hit property first. Check for collateral violations — resolve immediately.

flavourText: The buyers went on holiday. They haven't come back.

effects.description: ENERGY assets: subtract 1 from every value update die result (minimum 1). ENERGY's GMI-sensitive double-delta mechanic also applies on top.

flavourText: Prices fell. Everyone was surprised. Nobody should have been.

effects.description: The player with the highest total loan count gains +1 stress. Lenders are reviewing the most leveraged portfolios first.
All players must check for collateral violations at the start of this year (before value updates) as well as the end. Two collateral checks this year.

flavourText: The phone calls from the bank started Monday. By Friday, everyone had lawyers.

effects.description: FINANCE assets: the asymmetric double-delta mechanic activates this year even though GMI is not at depression level. Finance feels corrections before anyone else.

flavourText: The desk stopped picking up. Then the desk stopped existing.

effects.description: All asset value update rolls this year treat their result as 1 lower (minimum 1). A broad-based decline — no sector is spared.

flavourText: Everyone lost a little. Some lost a lot.

gmiDescription: GMI –2 this year. Single year only — recessions do not persist.

effects.description: All asset value update rolls treat their result as 1 lower (minimum 1).
FINANCE assets: the asymmetric double-delta mechanic activates. Finance collapses faster in a recession.

flavourText: Two negative quarters. One very bad year.

effects.description: All players check for collateral violations on all assets BEFORE value updates occur. Resolve all violations before proceeding.
All loan repayment rolls this year: trigger threshold one higher. Lenders are pulling back.

flavourText: The soft landing was cancelled.

effects.description: ENERGY: subtract 2 from every value update die result (minimum 1). GMI-sensitive double-delta also applies on top.
MANUFACTURING: subtract 1 from every value update die result (minimum 1). Input costs spike. Margins collapse.

flavourText: The pipeline closed. Everything else followed.

effects.description: Any player who owns a FINANCE asset gains +1 stress. Financial exposure becomes a liability in contagion.
FINANCE: subtract 2 from every value update die result (minimum 1). Double-delta asymmetric mechanic also activates.

flavourText: It started with one bank. It didn't stay there.

effects.description: No new loan tokens may be drawn this year. Existing loans may be repaid or restructured but no new borrowing is permitted. Credit markets are closed.

flavourText: The window is closed. Come back later. Maybe.

effects.description: All asset value update rolls treat their result as 1 lower (minimum 1).
The player with the most total asset value loses 1 point from their highest-value asset's current value. The richest players have the most to lose.

flavourText: The headlines did more damage than the fundamentals.

gmiDescription: GMI –2 every year this card is active.
persistenceRule: Leave this card face-up on the table. At the start of each subsequent year, roll d6: on 4–6 the depression ends and this card is discarded; on 1–3 it persists and all players gain +1 stress.

durationRoll.resultMapping: Each year: roll d6. 4–6 = depression ends. 1–3 = persists, all players +1 stress.

effects.description: FACE-UP PERSISTENT: all asset value update rolls treat their result as 1 lower (minimum 1) every year this card remains active.
FINANCE assets: double-delta asymmetric mechanic activates every year this card is active.

flavourText: This isn't a correction. This is a reckoning.

gmiDescription: GMI –2 every year this card is active.
persistenceRule: Leave this card face-up. At the start of each subsequent year, roll d6: on 5–6 the crisis resolves; on 1–4 it persists and all players gain +1 stress.

durationRoll.resultMapping: Each year: roll d6. 5–6 = crisis ends. 1–4 = persists, all players +1 stress.

effects.description: FACE-UP PERSISTENT: no new loan tokens may be drawn while this card is active. Each year it remains: all players with 3+ total loans must remove 1 loan token (paying $1 as normal).
All collateral violations resolved at the start of each year while active, before value updates.

flavourText: The number on the debt is larger than the number that exists.

gmiDescription: GMI –2 every year this card is active.
persistenceRule: Leave this card face-up. At the start of each subsequent year, roll d6: on 6 only the collapse ends; on 1–5 it persists and all players gain +1 stress. The hardest possible escape condition.

durationRoll.resultMapping: Each year: roll d6. 6 only = collapse ends. 1–5 = persists, all players +1 stress.

effects.description: FACE-UP PERSISTENT: all value update rolls treat their result as 2 lower (minimum 1) every year this card remains active.
All loan repayment rolls: trigger threshold two higher. Lenders are calling everything.

flavourText: The system didn't fail. The system did exactly what it was designed to do.

persistenceRule: Leave this card face-up. While active, the minimum delta outcome for all TECHNOLOGY assets is raised by 1 (e.g. a printed floor of –4 becomes –3; a floor of –1 becomes 0). When any GMI update is negative while this card is active, the bubble pops: remove this card, all TECHNOLOGY asset values drop by 3 immediately, and any player holding more than 2 loan tokens secured on TECHNOLOGY assets must remove 1 token per excess loan above 2 (paying $1 per removal as normal).

effects.description: TECHNOLOGY assets: add +2 to every value update die result this year. See persistence rule above — bubble floor protection is active until a negative GMI year triggers the pop.

flavourText: The revenue doesn't matter. The story does.

persistenceRule: Leave this card face-up. While active, the minimum delta outcome for all REAL_ESTATE assets is raised by 1 (e.g. a printed floor of –2 becomes –1). When any GMI update is negative while this card is active, the bubble pops: remove this card, all REAL_ESTATE asset values drop by 3 immediately, and any player whose total loans exceed 80% of their total REAL_ESTATE asset value must resolve a collateral violation immediately.

effects.description: REAL_ESTATE assets: add +2 to every value update die result this year. Bubble floor protection active — see persistence rule.

flavourText: It's different this time. They said that last time too.

persistenceRule: Leave this card face-up. While active, the minimum delta outcome for all ENERGY assets is raised by 1 (e.g. a printed floor of –3 becomes –2). When any GMI update is negative while this card is active, the bubble pops: remove this card, ENERGY's GMI-sensitive mechanic applies at triple (not double) this year, and any player holding more than 3 loan tokens secured on ENERGY assets must remove 1 token (paying $1) and gains +1 stress.

effects.description: ENERGY assets: add +2 to every value update die result this year. Bubble floor protection active — see persistence rule.

flavourText: The commodity supercycle is different from all the other supercycles.

effects.description: All players gain +1 stress immediately. No exceptions.
MEDIA_ENTERTAINMENT DOWNSTREAM (live events): subtract 2 from every value update die result (minimum 1). Live events cannot happen.

flavourText: The calendar cleared. So did the portfolio.

gmiDescription: GMI base 0. Roll d6: 1–2 = –1, 3–4 = 0, 5–6 = +1. Uncertainty more than damage.

effects.description: All players gain +1 stress immediately.
ENERGY assets: add +1 to every value update die result. Geopolitical instability drives energy price volatility upward.

flavourText: The conflict is far away. The fuel prices are not.

effects.description: All players gain +1 stress immediately.
All players pay taxes as if they earned $1 more this year. Tax authorities are expanding their definition of taxable income.

flavourText: The rules changed. Retroactively.

effects.description: Turn order is reversed for this entire year — all phases (auction, value updates, settlement) proceed in the opposite direction. The player who normally goes last goes first.

flavourText: The last shall be first. For now.

gmiDescription: The player with the lowest current score (Total Asset Value – Total Taxes Paid) sets the GMI delta for this year. They choose any integer between –1 and +3 and announce before value updates.

effects.description: Lowest-score player sets the GMI delta this year (choose –1 to +3). Announced before value updates. Final and binding.

flavourText: For once, the market listens to the little guy.

effects.description: Each player must immediately discard their current CEO card and draw a replacement at random from the available CEO tier they qualify for. New CEO effects begin this year.

flavourText: The board met. Nobody left happy. Nobody left as CEO.
